expect: Score all 18+ predictions; identify systematic bias pattern; Brier score estimate with CI
actual: All 18 predictions remain OPEN — zero resolved. Brier score cannot yet be computed from outcomes. Mid-cycle S513 EAD shows 2/7 trackable trending toward target, 4 flat, 1 against. Systematic biases identified: (1) BULL directional bias (10 BULL vs 5 BEAR vs 3 NEUTRAL = 56% BULL), (2) safe-haven narrative bias — highest-confidence cluster all predicts war→safety which is failing, (3) 8/18 missing baseline_price — data quality gap prevents half-portfolio P&L tracking, (4) confidence-conviction inversion: highest-confidence predictions are performing worst (PRED-0004 GLD 0.70→0.50, now worst performer -3.1%). PRED-0017 (SPY 1w) is 6 days from resolve_by with zero movement — trending toward INCORRECT. PRED-0003 (TLT 1m) and PRED-0018 (NVDA 1m) resolve 2026-04-21.
diff: Expected: ability to compute current Brier score. Actual: no resolved predictions yet — premature for scoring. The more important finding is pre-resolution bias analysis. Key surprise: safe-haven thesis is failing across ALL three predicted safe-haven assets simultaneously (GLD -3.1%, TLT flat, XLU -4.1% on Mar 20), suggesting the swarm's macro model has a systematic error — it conflates 'war narrative = risk-off = safety rally' but the actual market mechanism at scale is a dollar-liquidity squeeze that causes indiscriminate selling. The swarm is narrative-coherent but market-mechanism-naive.
method: Read registry.json (18 predictions, all OPEN); read S513 EAD checkpoint (f-fore1-ead-checkpoint-s513.json); read L-1410 (key finding lesson); analyzed confidence distribution, directional bias, thesis clustering, data quality gaps, and near-term resolve dates.

data.brier_score_note: Cannot compute — zero resolved predictions. Expected Brier from confidence distribution alone (assuming calibrated): avg_conf=0.537, E[Brier_correct]=(1-0.537)^2=0.215, E[Brier_incorrect]=0.537^2=0.288. At 50% accuracy: E[Brier]=0.251 — right at the coin-flip threshold.

data_quality_gaps.impact: Cannot compute P&L change for 44% of portfolio

systematic_biases.mechanism: Macro regime thesis (stagflation=defensive+energy BULL) generates more BULL than BEAR predictions; bearish predictions are only on broad market indices (SPY, QQQ)
systematic_biases.risk: If market rallies on ceasefire/Fed pivot, BULL predictions wrong across entire thesis

systematic_biases.magnitude: PRED-0003 TLT BULL, PRED-0004 GLD BULL, PRED-0009 XLU BULL — 3 predictions share same macro narrative
systematic_biases.mechanism: War→risk-off→safety rotation model. L-1410: this model is FAILING as of Mar 20. Market is pricing dollar-liquidity squeeze not safety rotation.
systematic_biases.risk: Correlated failure: if safe-haven thesis is wrong, 3+ predictions fail simultaneously. Not diversified.

systematic_biases.magnitude: Highest-confidence prediction (PRED-0004 GLD 0.70) is worst performer (-3.1%)
systematic_biases.mechanism: Swarm confidence correlates with narrative coherence and multi-domain convergence, not with proximity to actual market mechanism
systematic_biases.risk: Systematic overconfidence on thesis-coherent predictions. Calibration will likely be poor.

systematic_biases.magnitude: Most predictions cite geopolitical narrative (Hormuz closure) as primary driver without modeling market microstructure
systematic_biases.mechanism: Swarm has strong historical analogy capability but limited real-time market flow/liquidity modeling
systematic_biases.risk: Market mechanisms (margin calls, positioning, ETF flows) can override macro narrative for 1-3 month horizons

confidence_downgrade_history.reason: S513: GLD -3.1% vs +5% target; safe-haven thesis failing
S513: Relative trade 3.1% in wrong direction

expected_brier_scenarios.note: With mean confidence 0.537, swarm needs >50% direction accuracy just to beat coin-flip Brier threshold. If systematic biases hold, accuracy may be <50% due to correlated thesis failures.

verdict_rationale: Cannot confirm or falsify F-FIN4 yet — zero resolved predictions. However, mid-cycle indicators are unfavorable: highest-confidence cluster (safe-haven thesis) is failing, suggesting systematic model error rather than calibrated uncertainty. Directional bias (55.6% BULL) and correlated safe-haven cluster create tail risk of simultaneous multi-prediction failure. Expected Brier at current confidence levels requires >50% accuracy to beat coin flip — achievable in theory but the bias patterns suggest it will be difficult. PRED-0017 resolves in 6 days and is likely INCORRECT (flat movement, low confidence).
lesson_candidate: L-XXXX: Swarm prediction confidence correlates with narrative multi-domain convergence, not with market mechanism accuracy. In F-FIN4 mid-cycle, highest-conviction prediction (GLD BULL, 4/5 domain convergence on 'war=safety') is worst performer. This suggests a structural overconfidence bias: when multiple swarm domains confirm the same narrative, the swarm increases confidence — but domain convergence on a WRONG mechanism produces coherent overconfidence, not accuracy. Finance-specific: geopolitical macro narratives (war=safe-haven) can be overridden by market microstructure dynamics (dollar-liquidity squeeze, margin calls) at 1-3 month horizons. Calibration fix: require at least one domain to model market flow/positioning mechanics, not just narrative drivers.
